title: विष्णु मङ्गलाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु मङ्गलाष्टकम् ॥

श्रियःकान्ताय कल्याणनिधये निधयेऽर्थिनाम्।
श्रीवेङ्कटनिवासाय श्रीनिवासाय मङ्गलम्॥१॥

लक्ष्मीसवाय देवाय विष्णवे प्रभविष्णवे।
शङ्खचक्रगदापाणये नारायणाय मङ्गलम्॥२॥

श्रीवत्सवक्षसे नित्यं श्रुतिसागरमन्थन।
भवदुःखहराय विष्णवे मधुसूदनाय मङ्गलम्॥३॥

पद्मनाभाय देवाय पद्मनेत्राय शार्ङ्गिणे।
पद्मपादाय कृष्णाय गोविन्दाय नमो मङ्गलम्॥४॥

वैकुण्ठाय विरूपाक्ष पीताम्बरधराय च।
सर्वेश्वराय सर्वाय श्रीधराय च मङ्गलम्॥५॥

आदिदेवाय देवाय दैत्यदानवमर्दिने।
चक्राय विष्टरश्रवसे गदाधराय मङ्गलम्॥६॥

शेषपर्यङ्कशयनाय सर्वलोकेश्वराय च।
क्षीराब्धिवासिने देवाय माधवाय च मङ्गलम्॥७॥

परमात्मने परेशाय पवित्राय पिनाकिने।
श्रीमते नमो विष्णवे दामोदराय मङ्गलम्॥८॥

मङ्गलाष्टकमेतद्यो विष्णोर्भक्त्या पठेन्नरः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु मङ्गलाष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Mangalashtakam ||

Shriyah-kantaya Kalyana-nidhaye Nidhaye-arthinam.
Shri-Venkata-nivasaya Shri-Nivasaya Mangalam.||1||

Lakshmi-savaya Devaya Vishnave Prabhvishnave.
Shankha-chakra-gada-panaye Narayanaya Mangalam.||2||

Shrivatsa-vakshase Nityam Shruti-sagara-manthana.
Bhava-duhkha-haraya Vishnave Madhusudanaya Mangalam.||3||

Padmanabhaya Devaya Padmanetraya Sharngine.
Padma-padaya Krishnaya Govindaya Namo Mangalam.||4||

Vaikunthaya Viroopaksha Pitambara-dharaya Cha.
Sarveshvaraya Sarvaya Shridharaya Cha Mangalam.||5||

Adi-devaya Devaya Daitya-danava-mardine.
Chakraya Vishtarashravase Gadadharaya Mangalam.||6||

Shesha-paryanka-shayanaya Sarva-lokeshvaraya Cha.
Kshirabdhi-vasine Devaya Madhavaya Cha Mangalam.||7||

Paramatmane Pareshaya Pavithraya Pinakine.
Shrimate Namo Vishnave Damodaraya Mangalam.||8||

Mangalashtakam-Etadyo Vishnor-bhaktya Pathennnarah.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnu-sayujyam-apnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Mangalashtakam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. लक्ष्मीपति, कल्याणनिधि, याचकों के निधि, श्रीवेंकटेश, श्रीनिवास को मंगल हो।

२. लक्ष्मीवल्लभ, देव, प्रभुविष्णु, शंख-चक्र-गदाधारी नारायण को मंगल हो।

३. श्रीवत्सधारी, वेदसागर-मंथनकर्ता, भवदुःखहारी विष्णु मधुसूदन को मंगल हो।

४. पद्मनाभ, पद्मनेत्र, शार्ङ्गधारी, पद्मपाद, कृष्ण, गोविन्द को मंगल नमस्कार।

५. वैकुण्ठवासी, पीताम्बरधारी, सर्वेश्वर, श्रीधर को मंगल हो।

६. आदिदेव, दैत्य-दानव-मर्दन, चक्रधारी, गदाधर को मंगल हो।

७. शेषशय्या पर शयन करने वाले, सर्वलोकेश्वर, क्षीरसागरवासी माधव को मंगल हो।

८. परमात्मा, परेश, पवित्र, श्रीमान विष्णु दामोदर को मंगल हो।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस विष्णु मंगलाष्टक का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वपापों से मुक्त होकर विष्णुसायुज्य प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Invokes the auspicious grace of Lord Vishnu through eight mangala verses
Ideal for beginning any auspicious ceremony or ritual
Liberates from all sins and grants Vishnu Sayujya (union with Vishnu)
Traditionally associated with prosperity, protection, and divine blessings
Powerful on Ekadashi, Thursdays, and Vishnu festivals
Traditionally invoked to establish an atmosphere of divine auspiciousness
description: Vishnu Mangalashtakam (विष्णु मङ्गलाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Vishnu Purana. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke the auspicious grace of Lord Vishnu through eight mangala verses and bring prosperity, protection, and divine blessings. It is also said to create an atmosphere of divine auspiciousness. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ekadashi and Vaikuntha Ekadashi for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Mangalashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Mangalashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight auspicious verses invoking Lord Vishnu's blessings for prosperity and liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vishnu Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Mangalashtakam is a set of eight auspicious (mangala) verses praising Lord Vishnu and invoking his blessings for well-being, prosperity, and harmony. 'Ashtakam' means a composition of eight verses, and 'mangala' means auspicious — making this an octet of auspiciousness.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Vishnu Mangalashtakam chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Mangala stotras are traditionally chanted at the conclusion of any puja, parayana, or auspicious function — including weddings, griha pravesham, and Satyanarayan kathas. Vishnu Mangalashtakam is also recited on Ekadashi, Vaikuntha Ekadashi, and during Vishnu Sahasranama parayana as the closing prayer.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of chanting Vishnu Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The mangala verses are believed to invoke auspiciousness, ward off inauspicious influences, and seal the spiritual merit of any preceding worship. Traditional practice holds that chanting Vishnu Mangalashtakam at the end of any sacred function ensures that its blessings are stable and complete.

seoFaqs.question: What does Mangalam Bhagwan Vishnuh mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The well-known mangala verse 'Mangalam Bhagwan Vishnuh, Mangalam Garudadhwajah, Mangalam Pundarikakshah, Mangalayatano Harih' means: 'Auspicious is Lord Vishnu, auspicious is he whose banner bears Garuda, auspicious is the lotus-eyed one, auspiciousness itself is Hari.' It is the most commonly chanted Vishnu mangala mantra.

seoFaqs.question: Why are Mangalashtakams chanted at the end of pujas?
seoFaqs.answer: Mangala stotras seal a worship ritual with auspiciousness and remove any inadvertent errors in the procedure. By concluding with names of the Lord that embody mangala, the merit of the entire puja is offered to the Divine and stabilised — a core principle of all Vedic and Agamic ritual closure.
